title: terpsichore xanthotrichia
intro: Terpsichore xanthotrichia, also known as the yellow-haired vane fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Terpsichore xanthotrichia include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of allergies. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote respiratory health and relieve congestion.
